questions.text: A 63-year-old man is complaining of substernal chest pain radiating to his left arm and jaw. He rates his pain an 8 on a 1-10 scale. Baseline vital signs are: BP 136/70, P 126, R 16. The patient takes no medications regularly and has no allergies to medications. You should now:
options: Attach the external defibrillator pads to the patient's chest.
Contact medical control and request permission to administer nitroglycerin.
Ask your partner to assist the patient's respirations with a bag-valve-device.
Place the patient in a position of comfort and administer high-concentration oxygen by nonrebreather mask.

questions.text: Which of the following conditions is characterized by increased reactivity of the lower airway to irritants with reversible narrowing of the bronchioles and increased mucus production?
If need to move patient complain of inadequate breathing, you choose :-
Is the measurement of the force the blood exerts against the walls of blood vessels during the hearts contraction and relaxation:
What does the letter V represent in the muemanic memory device "AVPU":

63-year-old man is complaining of substernal chest pain radiating to his left arm and jaw. He rates his pain an 8 on a 1-10 scale. Baseline vital signs are: BP 136/70, P 126, R 16. The patient takes no medications regularly and has no allergies to medications. You should now :
options: Attach the external defibrillator pads to the patient's chest.
Contact medical control and request permission to administer nitroglycerin
Ask your partner to assist the patient's respirations with a bag-valve-device.
Place the patient in a position of comfort and administer high-concentration oxygen by nonrebreather mask.

questions.text: The single most important factor in determining survival from cardiac arrest is:
(The measurement of the force the blood exerts against the walls of blood vessels during the hearts contraction and relaxation) called:

options: Chest rises and falls with ventilation
The heart rate return to normal
The breathing rate is too fast or too slow

Hyperventilation lowers the levels of.

options: checks the integrity of the cuff

options: Atleast one fourth the depth of the chest, approximately 1 inches (2.5cm)
Atleast one third the depth of the chest, approximately 1.5 inches (4cm)
Atleast one half the depth of the chest, approximately 2 inches (5cm)
Atleast two third the depth of the chest, approximately 3 inches (8cm)

questions.text: What is the compression-ventilation ratio for 2-rescuer infant CPR
What is the preferred technique for providing chest compression during 2-rescuer CPR for an infant
What is the best action to relive severe chocking in a responsive infant
options: Kneel behind the infant and perform abdominal thrusts
Give 2 breaths, reposioning the airway after each breath
Begin 5 cycles of back slaps, followed by 5 chest thrusts

questions.text: What is the minimum amount of time for suctioning the airway
What is the correct airway to use on a person with intact gag reflex
How often should you deliver ventilation during respiratory arrest
During insertion, the distal end of the esophareal-tracheal combitube ends in the esophagus about:
Steps for insertion of the esophageal-tracheal combitube include:
options: Checking the integrity of the balloons, lubricate the tube and blind insertion
Choosing the appropriate size tube and blind insertion
Choosing the appropriate size tube, checking the integrity of balloons, lubricate, and blind insertion
Choosing the appropriate size tube, lubricate, blind insertion

questions.text: Which condition can cause an upper airway obstruction
After LMA is inserted what is your next step
After correct placement of the LMA, how should you secure the tube
options: Does not need to be secure
Holds the LMA tube with your hand

questions.text: What is the LMA size generally used for adult females
If a patient is not alert and is breathing less than 8 breaths a minute, the EMT-B should:
options: Give high concentration oxygen via non-rebreather mask
Ventilate with a positive pressure device and 100% oxygen
Give high concentration oxygen with nasal cannula

questions.text: After being awakened from her sleep because of coughing and shortness of breath, this 65-year old woman has called for an ambulance. You find the woman apprehensive and breathing with difficulty. She tells you she is being treated for hypertension and takes digoxin and a diuretic. Her pulse 122, BP 192/112, and respirations are 36/min. Her temp is normal. Diffuse rhonchi wheezes and rales are heard on auscultation of the chest. You note jugular venous distension when the bed is at a 45 degree angle. The most likely cause for this clinical picture is:
Which of the following of not an aspect of pulse evaluation
Which of the following are likely to influence a patient's blood pressure
What should you observe when trying to observe if rescue breath for an infant victim are effective
options: Visible rise of the stomach with each rescue breaths
Visible rise of the chest with each rescue breaths
Complete compression of the ventilation bag
Air leaking around the ventilation bag

questions.text: You are attending a 54-year old male who is complaining of dyspnea. He is two pack her day smoker who acknowledges he has a smoker's cough. Over the last few days of the cough has become more bothersome and the sputum is now yellow-green and streaked with blood. He appears flush, somewhat cyanotic and in obvious respiratory distress. His vital signs are: pulse- 112, BP-150/90, and respiration-38, temperature-102. On auscultation of the chest rhonchi and rales are heard on the right side. You should suspect he has:
If a victim of foreign-body airway obstruction becomes unresponsive. After you send someone to activate emergency response system, what is the next recommended action
When you are assessing cardiac patient PVC. Which condition you will intervene immediately
_____ respiration is characterized by rappid shallow breathing followed by apnea
Hyperventilation leads to which condition, except:
A patients upper airway obstructed with sputum. You insist the pt for coughing. While breathing which sound can hear:
What should you do when you are administering breath by using bag-mask for a child who is not breathing, but does have a pulse
options: Give breath at a rate of 1 breath every 3 to 5 seconds
Position the child on his or her stomach
Squeeze the bag as often as possible

questions.text: (The maximum volume of air the lungs will hold when a patient forcefully breathes in) called:
Which of the following is a sign of shock?
Which is not considered a type of shock?

You have been called for a patient with sucking chest wound the patient cough up red frothy blood and you can hear the sound of air going into and out of the wound as the patient breathes your best management for this patient after ABC :
options: Apply occlusive dressing and sealed on all four sides
Apply occlusive dressing but taped only on three sides

questions.text: The harsh sound that heard during breathing { usually inhalation} that indicates an upper airway obstruction called:
you are dispatched to a patient home while you arrived you found him in a difficulty breathing sitting up and nealing forward this position called:
The patient with COPD are switched to a hypoxic drive they breathe based on high levels of O2:
The side effects of the prescribed inhalers are increased pulse rate, tremor, nervousness, nausea.
Strokes occurs when the arteries in the heart become blocked or break.
The best sign of hypo perfusion is :
Some respiratory distress patients feel better if the head of the stretcher is raised so the body is at a 45- to 60-degree angle. This is known as:
A bag-valve-mask device with a reservoir and an adequate oxygen source (at least 15L/min) will deliver an oxygen concentration of:
An NPA shouldn't be used if ___ has been found in the patient's nose.
Intubations is recommended for patients with a Glasgow Coma Scale grade of:
A nasal cannula will deliver ___ L/min of oxygen in optimal conditions.
In ideal circumstances, suctioning of the adult patient should not exceed............seconds,
All patients must have a patent airway establish during the:
You should use a new oxygen cylinder when the old one reaches------psi.
An NPA shouldn't be used if -----------has been found in the patient's nose:
A pulse point in or near the trunk.
The number of times the heart beats in a minute.
Peripheral pulses A pulse point in an extremity.
The volume of air per breath.

Capillary refill The amount of time required to refill the capillary bed after applying and releasing pressure on a fingernail. normal capillary refill is less than 6 seconds. decreased capillary refill is a good indicator of shock in patients less than 6 years of age.

Recovery position The left lateral recumbent position for a patient to maintain an open airway by allowing secretions to drain by gravity and allowing the tongue from occluding the posterior aspect of the mouth.
A device that displays the proportion of carbon dioxide in exhaled air.
The respiratory system structures through which air passes.
Bag-mask A common ventilation device consisting of a self-inflating bag. A one-way valve, a mask, and an oxygen reservoirs.
Bluish discolorations of mucous membranes and skin caused by hypo perfusion of tissues.
Jaw thrust A method of opening the airway by displacing the jaw forward, used instead of the head-tilt chin lift in patients with suspected spinal injury.
Tidal volume is the amount of air:
options: Inhaled or exhaled during a normal breath
Left in the lungs after a forceful exhalation
One can inhale after a maximal inhalation

questions.text: An advantage of liquid oxygen (LOX) over gaseous oxygen is that:
options: The oxygen content in LOX is much higher
A larger volume of LOX can be stored in a smaller space
LOX is much cheaper than gaseous oxygen
There are no special requirements for LOX storage and cylinder transfer

questions.text: When delivering mouth-to-mask ventilations with supplemental oxygen:
options: It is not for the patient's chest rise to be visible
Slow the ventilatory rate to 1 breath every 8 seconds
Provide a minimum flow rate of 15-30 L/min

questions.text: A bag--mask device with a reservoir and an adequate oxygen source (at least 15 L/min) delivers an oxygen concentration of:
In ideal circumstances, suctioning of an adult patient should not exceed seconds.
The oropharyngeal airway should be measured:
options: From the corner of the mouth to the earlobe
From the tip of the nose to the tragus of the ear
From the tip of the nose to the corner of the mouth
From the tip of the nose to the chin

questions.text: While inserting the nasal airway, the beveled tip should be directed toward the:
A non rebreather mask deliver-----oxygen at a flow rate 15L/min in optimal conditions :
Normal respiratory rate in infant is:
Shock might best be defined as:
The maximum acceptable flow rate for a nasal cannula is ___ L/min
Your adult patient with chest pain has a blood pressure of 100/54,a pulse of 112 and has a history of angina. You should :
options: Administer the patient's prescribed nitroglycerin after consult medical direction.

questions.text: A 45-year-old patient complains of breathing difficulty and and chest pain. He will not tolerate a non-rebreather mask. You should
options: move the mask to a position of comfort
provide artificial ventilation with a bag-valve mask
use a pocket mask with supplemental oxygen

questions.text: Cardiac compromise commonly includes all of the following signs or symptoms EXCEPT
The best sign of hypo perfusion is a change in mental status of the patient:
An EMT responds to a cardiac arrest .the patient Hasn't(DNR)order. The EMT should:
Excitability and anxiety, increased heart rateand headache, consider Side effects Epinephrine:
Oxygenated blood is delivered to the fetus via:
You are examining a woman in her eighth month of pregnancy and discover that her blood pressure is 100/70, her heart rate is 90, and her respirations are 20. These vital signs indicate:
options: Moderate hypovolemia at this stage of pregnancy

questions.text: What modifications of the usual management of cardiac arrest should be made for a pregnant patient?
options: Deliver low-flow oxygen to prevent fetal oxygen toxicity
Administer vasopressors at double the usual dose
Perform cardiac compressions higher on the sternum
Deliver small boluses of crystalloids to prevent fetal pulmonary edema

questions.text: On 1-minute evaluation of a newborn infant, you note the infant has blue arms and legs, a pink trunk, and a heart rate of 120. He is crying loudly and moving all extremities. The patient sneezes when stimulated. This patient's APGAR score is:
When using a bulb syringe to suction an infant just delivered, which of the following should you suction first?
The assessment tool commonly used to evaluate newborns is the:
In comparison to an adult airway structure, a child has:
options: The narrowest portion of the airway at the cricoid ring
A lower larynx, located at C5 to C6

questions.text: A primary concern for paramedics at the scene of a rescue incident is:
options: Rapid access to the patient or patients
The safety of the rescuers and patients
Rapid extrication for ALS and surgical intervention
Needs assessment for equipment and manpower requests

questions.text: Abnormal, high-pitched, "musical" breath sounds that are often louder during expiration are called:
The most reliable indication that adequate tidal volumes are being delivered during artificial ventilation is:
All patients must have a patent airway established during the:
During the initial assessment, an irregular heart rate would most likely be discovered by:
Which of the following terms best describes "shock"?
You have delivered an infant who is not breathing. After suctioning and physical stimulation, there are no respirations. You should:
options: clamp and cut the umbilical cord
provide ventilations at a rate of 40 to 60/min
start chest compressions at a rate of 60/min

questions.text: You are preparing a mother and her newborn infant for transport when the placenta begins to deliver. What should you do to deliver the placenta?
options: Firmly grasp the umbilical cord and pull the placenta from the mother to help reduce her pain
Place a sterile gloved hand inside the vagina to slightly elevate the placenta and to reduce pressure on the cord
Help the mother hold her legs together to delay placenta delivery until you arrive at the hospital
Help the mother with delivery of the placenta. Bring the placenta with you to the hospital in a plastic bag

questions.text: After delivery, you see that the infant's face and trunk are cyanotic. The infant is crying and has a pulse of 158/min. You should:
options: cool the infant to increase its circulation
provide ventilations at a rate of 40/min
calm the infant to slow down its respirations
dry the infant and provide free-flow oxygen

questions.text: You have suctioned, dried, and wrapped the newborn in a blanket. The baby is still not breathing. You should stimulate the baby by:
options: cooling it off with saline-soaked sheets
clamping and cutting the umbilical cord
flicking the soles of the feet and rubbing its back
holding it with its head slightly higher than its body

questions.text: Which of the following, combined with a breech birth, puts the delivery of a newborn at a higher risk?
The newborn should be placed in which position until the umbilical cord is cut?
options: at the level of the vagina

questions.text: What care should you give a newborn that has a pulse of 90/min and respirations of 28/min?
options: Allow the mother to hold the baby to calm it
Administer free-flow oxygen at 10 to 15 liters per minute
Ventilate the baby with a bag-valve-mask (BVM) device at 60/min

questions.text: Pulmonary resuscitation is necessary for a newborn if:
options: the baby weighs more than 7 lb
the breathing rate is greater than 50/min

questions.text: Which of the following situations would indicate adequate breathing when you assess a newborn's breathing?
options: a delivery time of less than 1 hour
a newborn with a strong cry

questions.text: You have delivered a limp infant who is not breathing. You should first:
options: stimulate the infant by flicking the soles of the feet

questions.text: Why is it important to keep a newborn warm while providing treatment?
options: A newborn has difficulty regulating body temperature
It is easier for you to provide treatment in a warm environment
Oxygen will flow from an oxygen source with little difficulty in a warm environment
The warm environment will make the newborn sleepy, making transport easier

questions.text: A 35-year-old woman, 32 weeks' gestation, reports increased swelling in her hands and feet the last week and now complains of a sudden, intense headache. Her vital signs are as follows: pulse of 92/min; respirations of 24/min; and blood pressure of 144/92 mm Hg. You are concerned she has:
Calculate an APGAR score for the following infant. Body is pink with acrocyanosis. Pulse is 88/min and respirations are absent. Infant does not cry or move when stimulated, and muscle tone is weak.

You have just delivered a baby boy. His appearance is pink and his pulse is 148/min with slow respirations. He has a weak cry when stimulated and resists attempts to straighten his legs. His APGAR score would be?
A 56-year-old male sustains a gunshot wound to the abdomen. Vital signs are BP 74/32, P 136 present only at the carotid, and R 24 and shallow. The target of fluid resuscitation is:
An unconscious 15-year-old male was struck on the head with a baseball bat. His arms and legs are extended and his right pupil is dilated and fixed. You should:
options: Insert an oropharyngeal airway, administer oxygen, and restrict fluids
Apply a non-rebreather mask, restrict fluids, and administer Mannitol
Perform nasotracheal intubation, restrict fluids, and provide adequate ventilation
Perform bag-mask ventilation at 20 per minute

questions.text: Which of the following mechanisms of injury does not commonly cause damage to the spinal cord?
A 4-year-old female is unconscious after falling off a bicycle. Which of the following is the best method to open the airway?
You have placed an NPA in your patient and now observe mild hemorrhage from the nares. You should:
options: Immediately remove the NPA and pack the nose with gauze
Immediately remove the NPA and reinsert on the opposite nare
Leave the NPA in place so as not to disturb the clot or reactivate bleeding
Contact medical control as an NPA will not cause hemorrhage

questions.text: A 34-year-old man has a gunshot wound to the right groin area. Arterial bleeding, which cannot be controlled with direct pressure or tourniquet, is coming from the wound. The patient appears confused, diaphoretic, and has weak peripheral pulses. What is the appropriate fluid resuscitation regimen for this patient?
options: Intravenous fluid at a "keep open" rate
Intravenous fluid; give enough fluid to maintain peripheral pulses
Intravenous fluid at a "wide open" rate
No intravenous access should be established in this situation

questions.text: Which of the following findings would not make a patient difficult to ventilate with a bag-valve mask?
Routine use of hyperventilation in the traumatic brain injury (TBI) patient will:
options: Cause vasoconstriction and increased cerebral ischemia
Cause vasodilation and decreased intracranial pressure (ICP)
Cause an increase of end-tidal CO2

questions.text: Bag-valve-mask ventilation:
options: Rarely causes gastric distention due to low airway pressures
Is more effective in patients whose dentures have been removed
Is easily accomplished by one rescuer at the head
May exceed 60 cm H20 pressure in the airway

questions.text: A 25-year-old female was stabbed in the left arm. She presents with an altered mental status and signs of shock. Bleeding is controlled by direct pressure and use of a tourniquet. Which intravenous fluid therapy is appropriate?
options: Estimate blood loss and administer fluid at a 3:1 ratio
Fluid administration to maintain systolic pressure of 60 mmHg
Intravenous fluid administration bolus of 500 mL, then reassess

questions.text: A 57-year-old male has a respiratory rate of 36, ETCO2 of 30 mmHg, and an oxygen saturation of 80%. You should:
options: Coach the patient to slow his breathing as his ETCO2 level indicates hyperventilation
Ventilate with a BVM at a higher rate to increase the patient's oxygen levels
Ventilate with a BVM at a rate of 6 to increase the patient's ETCO2 level

questions.text: A 31-year-old male presents with difficulty breathing, rapid and weak pulse, flat neck veins, and midline trachea. Your assessment of the left side of the chest reveals decreased breath sounds and dullness to percussion. You should suspect:
Which of the following is not a desired characteristic of a suction device?
options: It can be carried in an airway kit with an oxygen cylinder and other airway equipment
It can be hand powered or battery powered
It can generate sufficient suction and volume displacement to remove pieces of food, blood clots, and thick secretions from the oropharynx
It can be powered by your portable O2 cylinder so it is not dependent on battery power

questions.text: When performing a needle decompression, which of the following is the best anatomical location?
options: Directly over the top of the fifth rib in the anterior axillary line
Directly over the top of the fourth rib in anterior axillary line
Directly under the bottom of the third rib in the midclavicular line
Directly over the top of the second rib in the anterior axillary line

questions.text: A 25-year-old female was stabbed in the left arm. She presents with an altered mental status and signs of shock. Bleeding is uncontrolled despite direct pressure. You should:
options: Apply a tourniquet directly over the wound
Elevate the extremity above the level of the heart
Apply a tourniquet proximal to the injury

questions.text: A 32-year-old male is involved in a motor vehicle collision. The steering wheel is bent. Your assessment reveals present and equal bilateral breath sounds and a rapid and weak radial pulse that disappears upon inspiration. You should suspect:
You have a patient with an isolated stab wound to the lateral chest. According to recent studies, which of the following procedures should be avoided?

A 34-year-old female was struck by a vehicle, causing blunt force chest trauma. She is unconscious with gasping respirations, almost no air movement, a rapid, thready carotid pulse and flat neck veins. You should:
A 34-year-old male is injured in a motor vehicle collision. He complains of diffuse abdominal tenderness. Vital signs are BP 100/60, P 120 and R 28 and shallow. You should:
options: Administer sufficient intravenous fluids to maintain peripheral pulses
Complete spinal motion restriction, start ventilations as needed, and transport immediately
Administer intravenous fluids to maintain a 130 mmHg systolic BP

questions.text: Which of the following indicates a state of hyperventilation?
options: An adult respiratory rate greater than 20 per minute
A tidal volume of less than 400 ml
An end tidal carbon dioxide level less than 30 mmHg

questions.text: An unresponsive 34-year-old female was struck by a motor vehicle. You observe asymmetrical chest wall movement with a flail segment on the right. Following delegation of c-spine control and opening the airway, you should:
Which of the following findings would indicate the airway needs to be suctioned?
options: Presence of dried blood in the nares

questions.text: Overinflation of an LMA mask can cause all of the following except:

A 54-year-old male is involved in a motor vehicle collision. The steering wheel is bent. During your initial assessment, you note his skin is pale, radial pulses are present and breath sounds are clear. Which one of the following is most consistent with these findings?
Which of the following is not a potential complication of performing chest decompression?
A 16-year-old female is rescued from a burning house. She has 25% partial thickness burns, and the burned areas are hot to the touch. What is the appropriate treatment?
options: Apply ice to burned areas until cool to the touch
Apply clean water to burned areas for up to 5-10 minutes
Apply iced water to burned areas until cool to the touch
Do not apply anything to burned areas other than clean sheets

questions.text: An unresponsive 5-year-old female was struck by a car. She presents with retractions and nasal flaring. The airway is patent, breathing is fast and shallow, and the carotid pulse is weak and slow. Which of the steps in her management should be done first?
During transport, a trauma patient develops shallow and rapid breathing, cyanosis and a weak, rapid carotid pulse with distended neck veins and diminished breath sounds on the right. You should:

A 23-year-old female fell from a second-floor balcony. Upon arrival, you find her lying in the grass. She responds to verbal commands and your assessment reveals flat neck veins, and normal chest, abdomen and pelvis examinations. Her skin is cool, clammy and ashen; respirations are rapid and shallow; radial pulses are too rapid to count and thready. You place her on the heart monitor and it shows a wide-complex tachycardia of about 280 per minute. You should suspect:
In which situation should an Emergency Rescue be performed?
options: Leaking antifreeze from a vehicle radiator

questions.text: Which of the following is the most common cause of abdominal trauma?
A 78-year-old female driver struck a parked car while she was traveling at a low rate of speed. She is complaining of shortness of breath and chest pain. Your assessment and treatment should include:
options: Breath sounds, history, place in a position of comfort

questions.text: You are delivering adequate ventilation volume when:
options: You provide 5-7 ml/kg per of ventilator support
Air begins to leak from the mask seal
You observe bilateral chest rise and fall

questions.text: An unresponsive 34-year-old female was struck by a car. You observe asymmetrical chest motion. Following delegation of C-spine control and opening the airway, you should next:
Which one of the following is typically associated with post-traumatic hemorrhage early shock?
options: Loss of 30% to 45% of blood volume

questions.text: A 52-year-old male has an isolated knee dislocation. He is alert and oriented, BP 144/84, P 88 and R 20. Pulses distal to the location are absent and you have an extended transport time. You should:
options: Splint the injury in the position found
Gently apply traction in an effort realign the extremity to restore pulses
Place patient in a Trendelenburg position
Flex the knee and splint to the opposite leg

questions.text: Which of the following is the most reliable early indicator for determining shock in the injured child?
When using positive pressure ventilation in a trauma cardiopulmonary arrest patient, you should ventilate:
options: 1 breath every 8 seconds with a tidal volume of 750 cc
12 breaths per minute with a volume of 750 cc
8 breaths per minute with a volume of 500 cc
1 breath every 6 seconds with a volume of 500 cc

questions.text: A 34-year-old male is injured in a motor vehicle collision and complains of diffuse abdominal tenderness. The initial assessment reveals fast and shallow respirations, a rapid, weak radial pulse, and pale, cool, diaphoretic skin with no visible external bleeding. You should:
options: Administer oxygen and perform a rapid trauma survey
Immediately evaluate his blood glucose and lactate levels
Complete spinal motion restriction and transport immediately
Obtain SAMPLE history, vital signs and cardiac monitoring

questions.text: An unrestrained 17-year-old female driver rear-ends a cement truck at a high rate of speed. She is unconscious, pale, cool and clammy, has fast, labored respirations and fast, thready radial pulses. Her neck veins are flat, trachea midline, chest motion is asymmetrical, and breath sounds on the left are absent. You should suspect:
The lateral approach for chest decompression is preferred:
options: When the access to the anterior approach is limited or unsafe
To minimize liver or spleen laceration
To help stabilize the catheter during movement or transport

questions.text: Which of the following is not caused by the release of catecholamines during shock?

An unresponsive 5-year-old female was struck by a car. She presents with retractions and nasal flaring. The airway is patent, breathing is fast and shallow, and the carotid pulse is weak and slow. You should:
Which of the following statements concerning treatment of shock in the pregnant burn patient is true?
options: Oxygen should be used sparingly so as to avoid oxygen toxicity to the fetus
Vasopressor agents such as dopamine should be used to improve circulation to the fetus
Volume replacement should be given earlier and in larger amounts to the pregnant burn patient
Volume replacement should be given more slowly so as to avoid fluid overloading the fetus

questions.text: A 49-year-old male was crossing the street and struck by a truck. First responders are doing CPR. The patient is pulseless, apneic and the monitor shows asystole. Your assessment reveals an open skull fracture and deformed pelvis. Based on the ITLS curriculum, which of the following is the most appropriate act?
options: Immediately intubate the patient to secure the airway
Establish intravenous access and administer a 1 liter bolus
Establish intravenous access and administer 2 mg of atropine
Resuscitative efforts should be discontinued, unless prohibited by law

questions.text: When performing a needle decompression, which of the following is the correct anatomical location?
options: Directly under the bottom of the second rib, midclavicular line
Directly under the bottom of the third rib, midclavicular line
Directly over the top of the fourth rib, midaxillary line
Directly over the top of the third rib, midclavicular line

questions.text: Which of the following assessment findings is associated with neurogenic shock?
options: Increased pulse, normal skin color and temperature
Decreased pulse, normal skin color and temperature

questions.text: Your first priority in the management of a trauma patient is:
options: Open the airway and assess for breathing
Provide ventilator support for your patient
Begin chest compressions if pulses are absent

questions.text: A 23-year-old female complains of the inability to feel her legs after falling down a stairwell. Her respirations are rapid and shallow; radial pulses are rapid and weak; and her skin is cool, clammy and ashen with no obvious external bleeding. She has flat neck veins and an unstable pelvis. You should suspect:

An unconscious 18-year-old male is involved in a motor vehicle collision. You find him unrestrained behind the bent steering wheel. He is unconscious with cool, pale and clammy skin. Vitalis are BP 90/40, P 120 and thready and R 30 and shallow. Your assessment reveals that he is blue around the lips, has distended neck veins and tracheal deviation to the right. He also has an asymmetrical chest with absent breath sounds on the left. You should suspect:

A 16-year-old male sustained a gunshot wound to the left chest and complains of shortness of breath. He presents with rapid and shallow respirations, absent radial pulses, and cool, clammy, pale skin. His neck veins are distended and there is an absence of breath sounds over the left side. You should suspect:
A 7-year-old boy has difficulty breathing and is wheezing. Vital signs are BP 94/62, HR 128, and RR 32. What initial management should be performed?
options: Open the airway with the jaw-thrust maneuver.
Place the patient in the Trendelenburg position.

questions.text: A newborn who was just delivered at home has cyanosis of the hands and feet, has a strong cry, and is moving her extremities vigorously. Vital signs are HR 164 and RR 52. What is the first step in managing this patient?
options: Dry her and then wrap her in a blanket.
Ventilate her with a bag-mask device.
Give high-flow oxygen by nonrebreather mask.
Maintain her airway using the jaw-thrust maneuver.

questions.text: What is the best way to obtain information about the medical history of a 6-year-old girl who is on a ventilator and has a tracheostomy?
options: Contact the hospital where she is usually admitted.

questions.text: In what position should the child in mild respiratory distress be placed?
options: On his or her right side.

questions.text: An 8-year-old boy has been sick for 3 days. He has been experiencing nausea and diarrhea that has become progressively worse. He is semi-responsive with pale and diaphoretic skin. It is difficult to obtain a BP. Other vital signs are HR 54 and RR 10. What type of shock is this patient experiencing?
When is it appropriate to use an AED in a witnessed pediatric cardiac arrest?
options: Once the patient is in the ambulance.
When the ambulance is en route to the hospital.

questions.text: A 7-year-old boy was having difficulty breathing during his physical education class. He appears tired and has audible wheezing. His vital signs are BP 98/66, HR 124 beats/min, and RR 32 breaths/min. What should the treatment of this patient include?
options: Initiating bag-mask ventilation at 12 breaths per minute.

questions.text: What is the first step in caring for a patient with a tracheostomy tube who is having difficulty breathing?
options: Administer a nebulizer treatment with a beta-agonist medication.
Place a mask over the mouth and initiate bag-mask ventilation.

questions.text: A 3-month-old infant is found unresponsive in his crib and has cool, mottled skin. His vital signs are HR 50 and RR 6. What is the initial treatment for this patient?
options: Establish IV access and administer an IV fluid bolus of 20 mL/kg.
Establish IV access and administer 0.01 mg/kg IV epinephrine 1:10,000.
Perform chest compressions and ventilate with a bag-mask device.
Initiate bag-mask ventilation and then reassess his skin color and heart rate.

questions.text: What is the initial airway management of a child who is seizing and has gurgling respirations?
A 4-year-old semi-responsive boy has had difficulty breathing and a fever for 3 days. Vital signs are BP 60/40, HR 128, and RR 10. What initial management should be performed?
options: Open the airway and maintain cervical spine immobilization.
Open the airway and ventilate with a bag-mask device.
Place the patient in the Trendelenburg position.
Apply oxygen by nonrebreather mask at 15 L/min.

questions.text: A newborn who was just delivered at home has cyanosis of the hands and feet, a weak cry, and vigorous movement of the extremities. Vital signs are HR 86 and RR 52. What is the most important step in managing this patient?
options: Dry her and then wrap her in a blanket.
Ventilate her with a bag-mask device.
Give high-flow oxygen by a blow-by device.
Maintain her airway using the jaw-thrust maneuver.

questions.text: A 2-year-old girl has been having respiratory distress for about an hour. She has crackles noted in her lower lobes and her lips are cyanotic. She is semi-responsive with pale and diaphoretic skin. It is difficult to obtain a BP. Other vital signs are HR 270 and RR 60. What type of shock is this patient experiencing?

An 8-year-old girl fell from a tree house that is approximately 10 feet from the ground. She is screaming and crying due to a deformity of her right lower leg. What is the appropriate initial management of this patient?
options: Consider preparing the AED and applying pads.
Cover her with a blanket and apply heat packs.

questions.text: What is the appropriate compression depth in a 6-month-old child in cardiac arrest?

A 2-year-old girl has been sick for 2 days. On arrival, she has a barklike cough and is extremely lethargic. Her vital signs are BP 66/P, HR 90, and RR 58. What should the treatment include for this patient?
options: Initiating bag-mask ventilation at 12 beats per minute.
Performing deep suction utilizing soft suction tubing.

questions.text: A 3-year-old boy with a tracheostomy is cyanotic and anxious after a recent tracheostomy change by a new home health nurse. After suctioning and attempting to ventilate with a bag, what should the provider consider?
options: Place a mask over the mouth and initiate bag-mask ventilation.

questions.text: After delivery of an unresponsive female newborn, you note she has central cyanosis and vital signs of HR 50 and RR 6. What is your initial treatment?
options: Establish IV access and administer an IV fluid bolus of 20 mL/kg.
Establish IV access and administer 0.01 mg/kg IV epinephrine 1:10,000.
Perform chest compressions and ventilate with a bag-mask device.
Initiate bag-mask ventilation and then reassess skin color and heart rate.

questions.text: What is the initial airway management of an unrestrained child in a motor vehicle collision who has blood and broken teeth noted in the airway along with gurgling respirations?

A 5-year-old boy is wheezing and complains of chest tightness. He is sitting upright, using accessory muscles to breathe. Vital signs are BP 92/62, HR 128, and RR 36, at oxygen saturation in 93%. What Initial management should be performed?
options: Open the airway with the jaw thrust maneuver.

questions.text: High pitched respiratory sound associated with airway constriction?
Which of the following findings is the first to present when a child is in a state of compensated shock?

A 2-year-old boy has moderate respiratory distress. He appears uncomfortable and has a respiratory rate of 28 breaths per minute with tracheal tugging and stridor. Which of the following is the best position for the patient?
A baby has just been delivered at home. She is crying and breathing but her feet are blue. After clamping and cutting the cord, what do you do next?
options: Intubate her with a bag-mask device.
Maintain her airway using the jaw-thrust maneuver.

questions.text: Which of the following is the best description of optimal ventilation volume for a 6-month-old child?
options: Ventilate until you see abdominal rise.
Ventilate just until the chest begins to rise.

questions.text: An 8-year-old boy has been sick for 4 days with vomiting and diarrhea. He has not tolerated any fluids for the past 2 days. He is disoriented and pale. His HR is 162 and RR is 12, and BP is unstable to obtain. What type of shock is this?
What is the 1st thing you do when a kid with a tracheostomy is in respiratory distress and his ventilator alarms are going off?
options: Administer high-flow oxygen via non-rebreather mask over the mouth and nose
Perform a blind finger sweep of the stoma
questions.explanation: Disconnect the ventilator first, then manually ventilate through the tracheostomy. This ensures oxygenation and rules out ventilator failure.

questions.text: A child has a heart rate between 180 - 188 beats/min with a narrow QRS and a strong pulse. Which of the following best describes his rhythm?

A newborn who was just delivered at home has cyanosis of the hands and feet, a strong cry, and vigorous movement of the extremities. Vital signs are HR 120 and RR 52. What is the first step in managing this patient?
options: Dry her and then wrap her in a blanket.
Ventilate her with a bag-mask device.
Give high-flow oxygen by nonrebreather mask.
Maintain her airway using the jaw-thrust maneuver.

questions.text: A 5 year old boy has wheezing and looks uncomfortable. What is the appropriate treatment?
options: Administer oxygen while preparing nebulized bronchodilator therapy
Place the patient in a supine position
questions.explanation: The initial management of a patient in respiratory distress is to ensure adequate oxygenation. Oxygen should be administered concurrently while preparing definitive bronchodilator therapy, unless signs of severe anaphylaxis are immediately present.

questions.text: What is one of the 1st signs of compensated shock in children?
questions.explanation: Tachycardia is an early, primary compensatory mechanism in pediatric shock. Hypotension is a late sign indicating the child has entered decompensated shock.

questions.text: A 9 year old looks uncomfortable with accessory muscle use and moderate trouble breathing. What position should you transport him in?
questions.explanation: Patients in respiratory distress should be allowed to assume a position of comfort (often seated or "tripod"), which maximizes their anatomical ability to ventilate.

questions.text: A newborn comes out with blue extremities but is breathing and has a heart beat. What is your first step?
questions.explanation: Blue extremities (acrocyanosis) are normal in a newly delivered infant with adequate breathing and heart rate. The priority is to prevent hypothermia by drying and warming the infant.

questions.text: A patient presents with a recent onset of shortness of breath with exertion. She is presently receiving chemotherapy for treatment of breast cancer. Your physical exam reveals jugular venous distention and muffled heart sounds. Of the following, the most likely etiology of these findings is:
A nasopharyngeal airway is most indicated to a patient with (in):
options: Respiratory distress and wheezing in all lung fields

questions.text: A patient presents complaining of a four-day history of worsening shortness of breath. The clinical exam reveals absent lung sounds on the left. He has a past medical history of CHF and mediastinal cancer for which he is presently receiving chemotherapy. Based on this information, which of the following is most likely the cause of his dyspnea?

Your patient presents with an altered mental status after an acute onset of sharp, right sided chest pain and increasing respiratory distress. Her skin is cool, cyanotic, and diaphoretic. BP = 86/60, HR = 118, RR = 24. This presentation is most consistent with?

A patient presents seated on a park bench with dyspnea. You note accessory muscle use and hear inspiratory and expiratory stridor. She has a weak, rapid radial pulse and her skin is cool, pale, and diaphoretic. Which of the following is the most likely cause of her symptoms?

A patient presents in bed unresponsive, warm to the touch. Vital signs are HR = 110, RR = 18 with normal tidal volume, BP = 118/78, Blood glucose = 900 mg/dL. Which of the following most correctly describes the pathophysiology of her problem?
options: Her pancreas does not manufacture any insulin.
Her pancreas does not manufacture any glucagon.
Her pancreas manufactures too much glucagon.

questions.text: A heavily sedated patient presents unresponsive and vomiting with gurgling respirations at a rate of 6/min. Of the following, your first most immediate action would be to:

A 64-year-old male with a history of COPD presents sitting in a chair complaining of chest pain and shortness of breath. He describes an acute onset of sharp chest pain located below his right nipple, that is non-radiating and a 5 on a scale of 1-10. Your physical exam reveals decreased lung sounds on the right with very minor diffuse wheezing in all fields bilaterally. Of the following, which is the most likely cause of the patient's condition?

A patient presents alert to pain only and breathing at a rate of 6/min with shallow tidal volume. You note peripheral cyanosis, hypotension, and an ETCO2 of 65 mmHg. The most appropriate way to administer oxygen to this patient is with a:

An 80-year-old female nursing home resident presents supine in bed and lethargic. The staff report a declining mental status over the last 12 hours since beginning antibiotic therapy for a UTI. Your primary assessment reveals an absent radial pulse, a weak and rapid carotid pulse, and cool, pale skin. Based on this information, place the following potential causes of her hypoperfusion in order from most likely to least likely:

A 52-year-old female presents confused but alert complaining of fatigue, tingling in her fingers bilaterally, and chest palpitations. She has a history of AMI, HTN, and renal failure and has missed her last two dialysis treatments. Vital signs are HR=102 and irregular, BP=142/90, RR=18. Of the following, which is the most likely?
A 16-year-old female presents complaining of difficulty breathing. Auscultation of lung fields reveals expiratory wheezing in all lung fields. Of the following, the most likely cause:

A 22-year-old male presents with an acute onset of difficulty breathing while eating lunch. Your clinical exam reveals inspiratory stridor and wheezes to all lung fields bilaterally. He has a past medical history of an allergy to nuts. Based on this information, place the following etiologies in order of most likely to least likely.
options: Anaphylaxis, partial foreign body airway obstruction, asthma
Asthma, anaphylaxis, partial foreign body airway obstruction
Partial foreign body airway obstruction, asthma, anaphylaxis
Asthma, partial foreign body airway obstruction, anaphylaxis

questions.text: Your patient is complaining of an acute onset of chest discomfort and shortness of breath. His vital signs are P = 116, RR = 18, BP = 108/63. His skin is pale, cool, and diaphoretic. Based on this presentation, the patient is most likely experiencing?

A 72-year-old male presents supine with fever, history of vomiting and diarrhea and now difficulty breathing. Vital signs are BP = 102/64, P = 104, RR = 20. His skin is dry and cool with mottled appearance. Your treatment should include:
options: Placing heat pack on the patient's armpits, groin, and neck.
Having the patient drink plenty salted water.
Covering the patient with fleece or light blanket.

questions.text: A 32-year-old male presents sitting at a dining table in obvious respiratory distress, with audible stridor. Bystanders report a sudden onset of respiratory distress while eating dinner. Your physical exam reveals a strong, tachycardic radial pulse and skin that is warm, slightly diaphoretic. Of the following, which is most likely cause?

A female patient presents complaining of shortness of breath and sudden onset of nonradiating, right sided chest pain, reproducible with inspiration. Given these findings, which etiology is most likely to be causing her symptoms?

A 78-year-old female presents with a sudden onset of respiratory distress, which came on while she was at rest. She is anxious and agitated. Vital signs are BP = 78/36, pulse = 110, RR = 22. Abnormal findings include crackles bilaterally, cyanosis. Which of the following medications would be most effective?

A 34-year-old female presents with dyspnea, audible wheezing and responds to painful stimulus. Her husband states that she used her inhaler multiple times without relief. Of the following, the best treatment would be?

A patient presents disoriented to place and time, with a two-day history of abdominal pain and excessive thirst and urination. Breathing is 20/min and deep and his blood glucose level is 421 mg/dL. Based on these signs and symptoms, prehospital care should include:
You are caring for a six-year-old patient with respiratory distress. The pulse oximeter (SaO2) reads 93%. Your management of the patient should include:
options: Withhold oxygen until the patient develops accessory muscle use
Contacting the patient pediatrician prior to transport
Administering a metered dose inhaler if prescribed to a parent or sibling
Administer sufficient oxygen to maintain a pulse oximeter reading of at least 94%

questions.text: Which of the following interventions should be performed first for an unresponsive patient with a suspected spinal cord injury?
You are caring for a 22-year-old female who complains of respiratory distress after exercising. She is alert, speaking full sentences, and has a persistent cough. She is most likely experiencing:
Your 5-year-old patient is unresponsive. A carotid pulse is present at 40 beats per minute. Breaths are shallow at six per minute. You should immediately:
options: Place the patient in the recovery position

questions.text: Which of the following medications is intended to decrease myocardial workload and increase myocardial blood flow?
Your patient is unresponsive following blunt trauma to the head. Which of the following is the EMT first priority for an unresponsive patient?
Which of the following is the most frequent cause of airway obstruction?
Continuous positive airway pressure (CPAP) can reduce respiratory distress by:
options: helping to keep the alveoli open

questions.text: Which of the following is true during inhalation of a spontaneously breathing patient?
options: There is a drop in pressure within the thorax
There is an increase in pressure within the thorax
Air is being pushed into the lungs
The diaphragm and intercostal muscles are relaxed

questions.text: You are called for a 6-month-old infant with respiratory distress. Which of the following is true about patients in this age group?
options: infants require forceful ventilations with the BVM
infants are more resistant to hypoxia than adults
infants rarely develop bradycardia due to hypoxia

questions.text: You are treating a patient with respiratory distress. He has a history of multiple myocardial infarctions. Lung sounds indicate pulmonary edema. You note the patient does NOT have any JVD or pedal edema. The patient is most likely experiencing:
The left side of the heart receives oxygenated blood from the:
Which of the following provides oxygenated blood directly to the heart?
Which of the following vessels carries deoxygenated blood?
Which of the following describes the purpose of administering aspirin to a suspected myocardial infarction patient?
options: Aspirin will eliminate cardiac related chest pain
Aspirin reduces platelet aggregation in the coronary arteries
Aspirin reduces the risk of metabolic acidosis

questions.text: How should you open the airway of an unresponsive patient with an unknown mechanism of injury?
Your patient is a construction worker who fell from a residential rooftop. He is responsive to pain. Breaths are shallow and irregular. You should:
options: provide artificial ventilations with a bag-mask device and high flow oxygen
obtain an SaO2 reading before determining if oxygen is necessary
apply a nonrebreather mask with high flow oxygen
apply a nasal cannula and reassess the patient

questions.text: Your pediatric patient has spontaneous respirations. This means the patient is breathing:
A nonrebreather mask should be administered to patients who:
options: are breathing adequately with signs of hypoxia

questions.text: You are dispatched to the local jail for a prisoner found unresponsive in her cell. Her airway is clear. Respirations are rapid and shallow. The pulse oximeter (SaO2) reads 90%. Your first action should be to:
Which of the following findings indicates partial obstruction of the upper airway?
To determine if CPR is indicated for an unresponsive patient less than one year of age, you should assess the:
You respond to a preschool facility for an ill child. Upon arrival, you find an unresponsive 8-month-old. The infant has a palpable pulse of 50 beats per minute. Your next action should be to:
options: open the airway and assess breathing

questions.text: You are treating a 35-year-old man who was stung by a bee. He is allergic to bees and has a prescribed epinephrine auto-injector. His breath sounds are clear and non-labored. His BP is 130/82. How should you manage this patient?
options: Administer the epinephrine and transport rapidly
Begin immediate transport and administer the epinephrine en route
Assess the patient for signs of hypoxia, administer oxygen as needed, and transport
Let medical control know your anaphylactic patient needs his epinephrine

questions.text: An elderly patient reportedly collapsed on a golf course almost 10 minutes ago. You assess her level of consciousness and determine she is unresponsive. Which of the following should you do first:
options: open the airway and insert an OPA
assess pulse and begin CPR if needed
providing ventilations with high flow oxygen

questions.text: You are caring for a 3-year-old patient in cardiac arrest. What is the correct compression to ventilation ratio for two-person CPR on this patient?
Shallow respirations and bradypnea will likely cause:
options: easier visibility of chest rise and fall
a rise in pulse oximetry (SaO2) readings

questions.text: In unresponsive patients over one year of age, you should palpate the:
Which of the following adult patients is breathing adequately?
options: a patient with respirations at 16 per minute and equal chest rise and fall
a patient with rapid, shallow respirations
a patient with shallow respirations at 8 per minute
a patient with snoring respirations and cyanosis

questions.text: An oropharyngeal airway is indicated for which of the following patients?
A 16-year-old male is unconscious following an assault. When you insert an OPA, the patient begins gagging. You should:
options: continue to insert the airway and prepare to suction as needed
remove the airway and prepare to suction as needed

questions.text: You are caring for a six-year-old patient with respiratory distress. The pulse oximeter (SaO2) reads 93%. Your management of the patient should include:
options: Withhold oxygen until the patient develops accessory muscle use
Contacting the patient pediatrician prior to transport
Administering a metered dose inhaler if prescribed to a parent or sibling
Administer sufficient oxygen to maintain a pulse oximeter reading of at least 94%

Your 5-year-old patient is unresponsive. A carotid pulse is present at 40 beats per minute. Breaths are shallow at six per minute. You should immediately:
options: Place the patient in the recovery position

Which of the following is the most frequent cause of airway obstruction?

options: helping to keep the alveoli open

Air is being pushed into the lungs
